reforms.description: The federal government has set aside funds for financing pilot projects in carbon capture and storage, aiming to achieve technological breakthroughs necessary for reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in carbon capture technology is expected to lead to innovations that reduce emissions, thereby supporting environmental sustainability and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has a plan to rein in emissions and has set aside funds for pilot projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant investment in a specific technology area that could lead to broader environmental and economic benefits.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has set aside funds for financing pilot projects in carbon capture and storage.

reforms.description: The Canadian government is focusing on reducing the economic burden of taxation, including significant cuts to corporate income and capital taxes, to improve business competitiveness and stimulate innovation.
reforms.package_name: Tax Reform for Efficiency and Fairness
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering taxes is expected to enhance incentives for investment and innovation, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Canadian government has set a high priority on reducing the economic burden of taxation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in tax policy aimed at enhancing competitiveness and fostering innovation across the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Canadian government has set a high priority on reducing the economic burden of taxation.

reforms.description: The federal government has introduced significant corporate income and capital tax cuts to improve business tax competitiveness, which includes a preferential tax rate for small firms and enhanced R&D tax credits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the tax burden on businesses, the reforms are expected to stimulate investment and innovation, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has implemented significant tax cuts as part of its tax reform agenda.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax cuts represent a structural change in the tax system aimed at enhancing competitiveness and fostering innovation, with significant implications for the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has encouraged parallel reforms at the provincial level, where a shift to balanced or surplus budget positions likewise had set the stage for tax cuts.

reforms.description: Canada's Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) tax credit scheme is one of the most generous in the OECD, aimed at encouraging private sector R&D investment by providing substantial tax assistance for eligible current spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The SR&ED tax credit incentivizes firms to invest in R&D, which can lead to innovation and productivity improvements, ultimately supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Canada's R&D credit scheme is noted as one of the top federal tax expenditures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SR&ED tax credit represents a significant structural support for R&D investment in Canada, influencing the overall innovation landscape and competitiveness of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Canada offers one of the most generous rates of R&D tax assistance among a large group of OECD and emerging market countries.

reforms.description: The 2008 budget eased the definition of what constitutes a 'small' firm for access to the SR&ED enhanced investment tax credits, raising the ceiling from CAD 15 million to CAD 50 million in taxable capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to tax credits for small firms, the reform encourages R&D investment, which can enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The definition of small firms was officially changed in the 2008 budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly broadens the eligibility for a key innovation funding mechanism, potentially increasing R&D activities among small firms.
reforms.source_quote: In the 2008 budget, the definition of what constitutes a 'small' firm was eased in the case of access to the SR&ED enhanced investment tax credits: the ceiling was raised from CAD 15 million of taxable capital to CAD 50 million.

reforms.description: In May 2007, Canada announced a new science and technology strategy, 'Mobilising Science and Technology to Canada’s Advantage', aimed at promoting world-class excellence and enhancing accountability in research and innovation.
reforms.package_name: Mobilising Science and Technology to Canada’s Advantage
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance Canada's competitive advantages through improved research and innovation, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was announced and has been shaping federal budgets and policy initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Canada's approach to science and technology policy, aiming to integrate and prioritize innovation across various sectors.
reforms.source_quote: In May 2007 Canada announced a new S&T strategy, Mobilising Science and Technology to Canada’s Advantage, which is based on four core principles: promoting world-class excellence, focussing on priorities, encouraging partnerships, and enhancing accountability.

reforms.description: Changes to the phase-out limits for access to the enhanced SR&ED benefits for small businesses were introduced in the 2008 budget to reduce disincentives to growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing disincentives for small businesses to invest in R&D, this reform encourages innovation and growth in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Changes were made in the 2008 budget to enhance access to SR&ED benefits.
reforms.importance_rationale: This adjustment to the SR&ED program is significant for small businesses, as it directly impacts their ability to invest in R&D and innovate.
reforms.source_quote: Changes to the phase-out limits for access to the enhanced SR&ED tax credits might be improved.

reforms.description: The Alberta government released a 10-year Strategy in January 2006, which includes an Oil Sands Industry Sub-Strategy to address labour-force challenges in the oil-sands sector, focusing on education, attracting immigrants, and enhancing community attractiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the supply of skilled workers, the strategy aims to enhance productivity and support the growth of the oil sands industry, which is crucial for Alberta's economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was released and is being executed to address labour challenges.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant effort to tackle labour shortages in a key sector, which is vital for economic growth in Alberta.
reforms.source_quote: The government of Alberta released a 10-year Strategy in January 2006, which includes an Oil Sands Industry Sub-Strategy.

reforms.description: The Alberta government has signed an agreement with federal authorities to allow for the targeted entry of temporary foreign workers to meet urgent skilled-labour needs in the oil-sands sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating the entry of skilled workers is expected to alleviate labour shortages and support the expansion of the oil sands industry, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The agreement has been signed and is in effect to address skilled-labour shortages.
reforms.importance_rationale: This agreement is a significant step in addressing immediate labour needs in a critical sector, enhancing the capacity for growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Alberta government has also signed an agreement with the federal authorities that will allow for the targeted entry of temporary foreign workers.

reforms.description: The federal government unveiled the Clean Air Regulatory Agenda, which includes mandatory reduction targets for major industries producing greenhouse gases (GHGs) and aims to reduce Canada's total GHG emissions by 20% by 2020 and 60-70% by 2050.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The agenda promotes the development and adoption of cleaner technologies, which can enhance productivity and environmental sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has committed to specific GHG reduction targets and has begun implementing the agenda.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Canada's approach to environmental regulation, moving from voluntary measures to mandatory targets, which is expected to have a profound impact on emissions reduction and innovation in clean technologies.
reforms.source_quote: On 26 April 2007, the federal government unveiled the Clean Air Regulatory Agenda, designed to address climate change and air pollution.

reforms.description: The federal government announced new measures in the 2008 federal Budget to promote carbon capture and storage, support nuclear energy, and invest in research and development projects in the automotive sector for greener vehicles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in R&D for cleaner technologies can lead to innovation, enhance competitiveness, and contribute to sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget included specific allocations for R&D projects aimed at reducing environmental impacts.
reforms.importance_rationale: These measures represent a significant investment in R&D for cleaner technologies, which can drive innovation in the automotive sector and contribute to environmental goals.
reforms.source_quote: The 2008 federal Budget includes new measures to promote carbon capture and storage and support nuclear energy and invest in research and development projects in the automotive sector for greener and more fuel-efficient vehicles.

reforms.description: The federal government announced a focus on funding priority areas in its Science and Technology Strategy, aiming to integrate energy and environmental concerns into the national innovation system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By prioritizing funding in key areas, the strategy aims to enhance innovation capacity and address pressing environmental challenges, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government indicated its willingness to focus funding on priority areas.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding priorities, which could lead to enhanced innovation in critical sectors.
reforms.source_quote: In its Science and Technology Strategy, the federal government indicated its willingness to focus funding on priority areas that are in the national interest from a social and economic perspective.

reforms.description: A fund of CAD 550 million was established to support environmental innovation projects at the demonstration and pre-commercialisation stages, addressing funding gaps prior to venture capital investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing funding at critical stages of innovation, the fund aims to facilitate the transition of projects to market readiness, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Fund has been successful in providing support to 133 projects involving over a billion dollars of investment thus far.
reforms.importance_rationale: The fund addresses a significant funding gap in the innovation pipeline, particularly for small and medium-sized firms, enhancing their capacity to innovate.
reforms.source_quote: A fund of CAD 550 million provided to Sustainable Development Technology Canada was established in 2002 to address the gap in funding at the demonstration and pre-commercialisation stages.

reforms.description: The 2007 federal budget established a Major Projects Management Office to streamline the approval process for energy projects, enhancing regulatory efficiency and accountability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the efficiency of project approvals, the initiative aims to reduce delays and uncertainties, thereby facilitating investment and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The 2007 federal budget took an important step in addressing the issue by putting in place a Major Projects Management Office.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant improvement in regulatory processes, which can enhance the overall investment climate for energy projects.
reforms.source_quote: The 2007 federal budget took an important step in addressing the issue by putting in place a Major Projects Management Office.

reforms.description: The federal government announced the creation of the ecoENERGY for Biofuels Initiative, which will invest up to CAD1.5 billion over nine years to boost Canada’s production of biofuels such as ethanol and biodiesel.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to enhance the production of renewable energy sources, contributing to sustainable energy development and reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was announced with a specific funding commitment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in renewable energy, aiming to transform the biofuels sector and contribute to Canada's energy sustainability goals.
reforms.source_quote: On 5 July 2007, the federal government announced the creation of the ecoENERGY for Biofuels Initiative, which will invest up to CAD1.5 billion over nine years to boost Canada’s production of biofuels such as ethanol and biodiesel.

reforms.description: The government is committed to facilitating the commercialisation of cellulose ethanol, with the 2007 federal Budget allocating CAD500 million for large facilities producing 'next generation' renewables.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By investing in the commercialization of advanced renewable technologies, this initiative aims to enhance energy sustainability and reduce reliance on fossil fuels.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget allocation for this initiative was clearly stated.
reforms.importance_rationale: This significant funding commitment is aimed at advancing the commercialization of innovative renewable energy technologies, which is crucial for achieving long-term sustainability in the energy sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government is committed to facilitating the commercialisation of cellulose ethanol, and the 2007 federal Budget allocated CAD500 million for large facilities producing 'next generation' renewables.

reforms.description: The Canadian government introduced the Agricultural Policy Framework (APF) in 2003 to reshape agriculture policies, focusing on risk management, technology, and innovation to ensure long-term sector viability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The APF aims to enhance the agricultural sector's competitiveness through innovation and technology, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The APF was intended to provide a comprehensive and long-term basis to reshape Canada’s agriculture policies.
reforms.importance_rationale: The APF represents a significant structural change in agricultural policy, emphasizing innovation and technology to address competitiveness and sustainability challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The APF was intended to provide a comprehensive and long-term basis to reshape Canada’s agriculture policies, covering a wide range of issues such as risk management, long-run sectoral viability through technology and innovation.

reforms.description: In 2008, the Canadian government launched the Growing Forward initiative to succeed the APF, focusing on innovation and science to help the agriculture and agri-food industry seize evolving market opportunities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the agricultural sector's adaptability and competitiveness through a stronger emphasis on innovation and market opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: The new initiative called Growing Forward was launched after broad and multi-phased national consultations.
reforms.importance_rationale: Growing Forward represents a major shift in agricultural policy, emphasizing innovation and adaptability to global market changes, which is crucial for the sector's long-term sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The new framework will focus more on ensuring that the agriculture and agri-food industry can seize on evolving market opportunities in the global context, with a greater emphasis on innovation and science.

reforms.description: The Agriculture Policy Framework (APF) was implemented to reshape Canada's agricultural policies, focusing on long-term viability and competitiveness, including a strong emphasis on science and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The APF aims to enhance the growth and profitability of the agricultural sector through innovation and technology, which can lead to increased productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The APF was said to be designed to change Canada’s agricultural policy from simply focusing on income support to a more comprehensive approach.
reforms.importance_rationale: The APF represents a structural change in agricultural policy, integrating various aspects of risk management, food safety, and innovation to enhance the sector's competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: In essence, the APF was said to be designed to change Canada’s agricultural policy from simply focusing on income support to a more comprehensive approach that would include not only risk management but also the long-run viability of the agriculture and agri-food industry through technology and innovation.

reforms.description: The Canadian government launched an initiative to mandate a 5% ethanol blend in gasoline by 2010 and a 2% biodiesel blend in on-road diesel and heating oil by 2012, aiming to expand bio-energy production.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative is expected to create new markets for agricultural commodities and reduce reliance on fossil fuels, contributing to environmental objectives.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to mandate ethanol and biodiesel blends was publicly announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards renewable energy in the agricultural sector, with potential long-term impacts on market dynamics and environmental sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The Canadian federal government launched an initiative to mandate a 5% ethanol blend in gasoline by 2010 and a 2% biodiesel blend in on-road diesel and heating oil by 2012.
